 Operation Manual

Preface
Thank you for choosing our injection molding machine. In order to make you get
familiar with, understand and operate the machine better, please read this manual
carefully before using the machine.
This manual describes the necessary elements about safety operating the machine
and the explanation of the product processing. It includes the installation, operation
and transportation of the equipment, and provides the configuration instructions of the
machine.
This manual also includes the operating documents of the machine, and describes the
list of controller instructions and other technical data provided with the machine. The
owner of the machine should verify other data files according to the list.
Compiling Principles of the Manual:
Strict compliance with the items listed in this manual can help the user smoothly use
the machine and ensure the personal safety of the operator.

GuangDong Multitech Machinery Co., Limited
No. 32 Shengqi Road, Shahukou Village, Changping Town, Dongguan
City, Guangdong Province
TEL：0769-81893322

Chapter I Safety
1. Importance of Reading and Holding the Instruction Manual
Before operating the injection molding machine, be sure to read this instruction carefully to
avoid dangerous accidents. This instruction manual contains important information such as
safety precautions, operation and maintenance. If there is any problem that cannot be solved
in this manual, please contact your dealer or contact us directly, and we will serve you
immediately. The company will not assume any responsibility for any accident or damage to
the injection molding machine caused by the operation method not in accordance with this
Operating Manual, omission of inspection before operation, or simple transformation of the
injection molding machine and change of electrical circuit.

2. Special Safety Warning
(1) This injection molding machine is a vertical directly clamping type injection molding
machine.
(2) Type of hydraulic oil used in this machine: ISO Vg32, 32-62CST/40 degrees (SUS
200-300/37.8 degrees) R46.
(3) When the machine is replaced with new hydraulic oil, the old oil in the oil tank must be
completely pumped out. New and old hydraulic oil cannot be mixed together.
(4) When handling the injection molding machine, the pressure in the oil tube must be
reduced, so the hydraulic oil in the oil tube must be discharged.
(5) The machine must be grounded to the ground. Please pull the grounding wire from the
grounding terminal in the electrical cabinet to connect with the ground.
(6) Use well insulated cables and provide sufficient power supply for the injection molding
machine. The power supply must be supplied to the injection molding machine via the
circuit breaker.
(7) To extend the span life of the machine, please install a cooling system.
(8) Make sure all pipelines and circuits are firmly connected.
(9) Please follow correct operating procedures for the span life of the machine.
(10) Personnel operating the machine must receive educational training on injection molding
machine before operating the machine.
(11) Any change or modification of the structure of the machine or all components must be
approved or authorized by MULTITECH. It is not allowed to change any part of the
injection molding machine due to production requirements. MULTITECH will not be
5

Operation Manual

responsible for the installation of surrounding mechanical equipment affecting the
normal operation of the molding machine.
(12) Before operating the machine, please check that all safety devices are in normal
operation. It is forbidden to move or remove any safety devices. Confirm the emergency
button switch function is normal. Be sure to confirm that the motor will stop running
when the safety door is opened.
(13) Before operating the machine, please check that all safety devices are in normal
operation. It is forbidden to move or remove any safety devices. Confirm the emergency
button switch function is normal. Be sure to confirm that the motor will stop running
when the safety door is opened.
(14) When the machine is running, do not open the fixed protective cover. Turn off the power
before opening or removing the fixed protective cover.

3. Mechanical Hazards and Prevention
Confirm that all safety devices work properly and keep the following four principles in mind
during operation. These four principles are the most basic guidelines to ensure operator
safety.
The four principles of safety:
(1) Before reaching the clamping part, the safety door should be opened.
(2) When the body enters between the upper and lower mold plates, the motor should be
stopped at any time.
(3) Do not enter any moving parts of the device.
(4) Do not touch the heating ring or surroundings with bare hands.

6

 Operation Manual

4. Electrical Hazards and Prevention
(1) The machine must be connected to the correct power supply for operation. (Install
voltage stabilizing equipment if necessary)
(2) The machine must be grounded before operation, and the grounding mode should meet
the national or local standards. The ground wire end must be connected to the qualified
(do not less than 6mm2) protective conductor end (Preferably directly connected with
the ground wire of the power supply equipment).
(3) The grounding wire adopts the third grounding method, and the specifications of the
third grounding wire are detailed in the following table:

(4) If each electrical component is provided with a safety grounding wire, it must be
independently connected to the machine grounding terminal.
(5) The electrical box should have sufficient heat dissipation efficiency to prevent fire when
exposed to heat.
(6) Suitable and qualified wires should be selected for electrical wiring. (Including shielded
wires)
(7) Try to separate the wiring of high and low voltage wires as much as possible to avoid
interference and identification difficulties.
(8) The installation position of electrical components shall not be subject to mechanical
impact. (Especially the operation panel)
(9) The operation panel should not be collided by sharp or hard objects to avoid damage
and electric shock.
(10) The operation panel, host box and electrical components should not be wet, which may
cause short circuit and explosion.

7

5. Safely Prevent the Damage to the Operator caused by the Processed
Articles
Generally, the heated resin is in a very dangerous state. Not only due to high temperature,
but also due to the possibility of thermal decomposition or generated gases flying or spraying
out from the opening, this danger should be fully understood and the following points should
be followed:
(1)

Carefully investigate the melting temperature of the resin used and set the heating
ring at the proper temperature.

(2)

Warm up before operation. Do not let the machine stay in a high temperature
state for a long periods.

(3)

Be careful not to work near the opening of nozzle and hopper.

(4)

Do not heat up or operate when the hopper is unloaded.

(5)

Do not approach the nozzle and hopper with your face and hands during heating
and operation of machine.

(6)

When inspecting and cleaning the inside of the nozzle and hopper, wear safety
protection appliance on the face, hands and arms.

(7)

When removing nozzle, piston, plunger, and etc., the molten resin at a high
temperature may flow out or spray out. Do not touch the high temperature resin
with bare hands. Repeat the cleaning action until the residual resin is completely
discharged. The cleaning speed should be slowed down when the heating is over
or the cleaning is carried out after a long time interval.

(8)

Polyethylene, polyacetal and other easily thermally decomposed resins should be
cleaned and replaced with high-density polyethylene and other safety resins at the
end of the operation.

(9)

During the movement of the injection device, hands may be clamped by the nozzle
and mold. Do not remove resin droplets from the nozzle by hand.

(10)

Some resins will produce corrosive gases (POM, PBT, etc.). It’s necessary to
understand the characteristics of the resins used. If the resin produces gases, an
air exchange device should be installed for fully air exchange.

(11)

Due to different characteristics of different types of resin, the treatment methods
may vary when consumed or inhaled. (In some cases, water cannot be consumed)
Injection molding operation should be carried out on the basis of being familiar
with the characteristics of the resin.

8

Operation Manual

6. Noise, Fire Hazards and Prevention
When the machine has abnormal noise, please immediately check
the entire machine, especially the hydraulic pump, drive motor and
injection unit.

Noise

Motor
Oil Pump
Do not place combustible materials near the machine.
Due to the molten and cleaning resin flowing out from the nozzle are
still very hot, do not put any inflammable or dangerous goods (such
as diluents and alcohol) within the moving range of the nozzle.
Otherwise, it may cause fire.
Do not leave the machine when the resin is retained in the barrel.
Depending on the different type of resin, if the heater is placed in
the ON state, the resin retained in the barrel will be decomposed
Notes on fire and explosion

and cause explode.
When leaving the machine, be sure to remove the resin inside, and
then replace it with resin with better thermal stability (PP, PE, etc.)
before cutting off the power supply of the heater.
Pay full attention to the treatment of cooling water flowing around
the injection molding machine.
May cause of fire, fault, or electric shock.
Try not to use open flames near the injection molding machine.
If you have to use an open flame, be sure to confirm the complete
flameout.

7. Electromagnetic and Other Radiation Hazards and Prevention
Some devices such as cardiac pacemakers, hearing aids, and etc. will be
affected near magnetic mold fixtures.
Influence of magnetic force Pay full attention and caution to use these devices.
Avoid placing the machine in areas with high electric and magnetic
field.

8. Personal Protection Requirements When Operating the Machine
During injecting operation, wear specified outfits which are the most suitable for the working
environment.

Safety helmet
Gloves (if needed)
Working outfit (Button fastened)

Safety shoes (if needed)

In cases that insufficient drying, deterioration, and decomposition of materials may result in
spraying from the nozzle, it is necessary to wear appropriate protective equipment.

9. Safety Warning Signs of Machine
The machine signs include "DANGER", "WARNING", "CAUTION" and "NOTICE".
DANGER: It refers to an immediate dangerous situation that, if not avoided, will cause death
or serious harm to human body.
This restriction applies to extreme hazardous situations.
WARNING: It refers to a potentially dangerous situation, which may cause death or serious
damage if not avoided.
CAUTION: It refers to a potentially hazardous situation which, if not avoided, may cause
minor or moderate injury to personnel.
NOTICE: It refers to the danger to the injection molding machine only in case of an accident.

Chapter II Technology
1. Brief Description of Machine Unit

The above external machine name diagram shows the names of the external parts of the
machine, and the following describes the main units.
(1) Injection unit
a. The plastic required for melting should take less time than the cooling time of the
product during the melting process, so as not to extend the injection molding cycle.
Melting temperature should be at least high enough to fill the mold cavity with
material. The melting temperature should be kept as low as possible within the
allowable range in order to achieve a shorter cooling time, i.e. a short molding
cycle.
b. Keep the nozzle in close contact with the mold feed nozzle to avoid material
leakage.
c.

Inject the melted material into the mold cavity at a certain pressure and speed, and
perform pressure holding and molding.

d. Composition of injection unit: hopper, screw, barrel, rocket head, nozzle, screw
motor, cylinder position ruler, injection cylinder (screw cylinder), injection unit and
injection unit moving cylinder (unit cylinder). Refer to injection cylinder and
injection unit cylinder assembly diagrams.

15

 Operation Manual

Granular plastics enter the barrel from the hopper. When the molten material reaches
the top of the screw through the three-stage feeding (homogenization, compression,
and metering) of the plastic in the barrel, the injection cylinder drives the screw to be
injected into the mold cavity through the nozzle. The annular electric heating ring
surrounding the barrel and nozzle helps to melt the plastic, while the remaining heat
comes from the frictional shear force when the screw rotates. During feeding, the
screw is pushed backward due to rotation, and moves forward during injection. The
screw is respectively driven by a hydraulic motor and a hydraulic cylinder (injection
cylinder).
(2) Clamping unit
The clamping unit is an important component of this machine, which ensures the
reliable locking of the injection mold to resist the pressure of the mold cavity, open and
take out the components of the product. The clamping unit of the machine adopts full
hydraulic clamping (Vertical clamping).
Composition of clamping unit: clamping hydraulic cylinder (clamping cylinder),
clamping base plate, upper clamping plate, lower clamping plate, tie bar (refer to
Appendix Assembly Drawing of Die Cylinder and Machine Assembly Drawing) and
ejection units (refer to Appendix Assembly Drawing of Ejector Pin Units).
The maximum clamping force for the clamping unit of the injection molding
machine can exert on the mould. It is caused by the mould cylinder fixed on the
clamping base plate pushing the upper mould to tie bar. Tie bar provides reaction force
to the clamping cylinder. The clamping unit is used to fix the mould, make the moving
mould open and close, and lock the mould. When the mould is closed, the rated
clamping force will be generated under the action of clamping cylinder pressure to lock
the mould and prevent the mould cavity from expanding when the mould is injected
with high pressure melt. A position ruler is installed on the clamping base plate to
control clamping position.
Mechanical ejector unit are installed on the left and right sides of the moving mold
plate (sliding plate). When the mould is opened, the product can be ejected from the
mould cavity through the ejector unit in the mould. The ejector unit of the machine is a
hydraulic ejection unit. The moving stroke of the piston rod of the ejector pin cylinder is
the ejection stroke. The stressed area of the piston determines the ejector force. Limit
components (controlled by switch or position ruler) should be installed for the ejection
or eject backward of the ejection unit.
The safety door can be selected at the front, back or side of the clamping unit. In
order to ensure safety, the electric limit switch is interlocked with the safety gate. The
16
Operation Manual

clamping action can be carried out only when the safety gate is closed.
(3) Moving mold unit
According to different types of machines, it can be divided into S - single slide table
series, SD – integrated double slide tables series, SDS - split double slide table series, R
– rotary table series, and etc. The main function of the moving mold unit is to move out
the die, facilitate the inserts, easy to remove finished products and improve the
productivity. It is mainly composed of slide tables, slide cylinder or turntable assembly
(rotary table). See the appendix for slide tables assembly, slide cylinder assembly or
rotary table assembly drawings.
(4) Hydraulic system
The hydraulic system is the "blood" circulation system of current injection molding
machine, which is mainly composed of oil tank, oil pump, valve, oil circuit integrated
plate, cooler, pipeline, and etc. The oil pump is the power source of the hydraulic
system, providing the required pressure oil for the action of the actuator such as the
hydraulic cylinder and the hydraulic motor. The hydraulic motor is a power unit that
outputs speed and torque under the action of pump output hydraulic oil. Motor drives
screw rotates. The hydraulic cylinder converts the oil power into push-pull and linear
actions for die opening and closing, injection and ejection. The main function of the
valve is to control the operating pressure and speed (flow rate) of each actuator
(hydraulic cylinder and hydraulic motor). 3 mainly types of valves: pressure control
valve, flow control valve and direction control valve. (See Appendix Oil Circuit Diagram
for details).
(5) Electrical system
It consists of three-phase motor driving the oil pump, motor starters, a
ring-shaped heater that maintains the temperature of the barrel and nozzle, relay and
other electrical components.
(6) Electronic control system
The electronic control system is the "central nervous system" of injection molding
machine, which controls the sequence of injection cycle (sequence control) and
maintains the process temperature, time, pressure and speed at the set value (program
control). The control system of the machine is controlled by the computer. The control
is displayed on the screen, which is reliable and safe. The abnormal situation can
display on the screen. The electronic control system and mechanical hydraulic system
are combined to precisely and stably control the process of injection molding machine.

2. Injection Molding Problems and Solutions
17

 Operation Manual

(1) Surface quality of injection molding products
In the product structure, the status of plastic products is increasingly improved, and
injection molding products are widely used in engineering, home appliances and
decoration. The reason why the surface quality of injection molding products is
important is not only because the surface defects will affect the aesthetics of the
products to make the owners unacceptable, but also the injection molding products
should have the same quality as inside: many apparent defects are because of
unreasonable injection molding process conditions, polymer materials and mold
selection; these reflect the internal quality of products. Therefore, exploring the causes
and elimination methods of surface quality is a great significance to control the external
and internal quality of products.
Apparent defects of products include the following 9 aspects:
a. Dent, shrinkage cavity and air void
Causes:
Most of these defects are caused by high moisture absorption of raw materials, poor drying,
uneven product wall thickness, insufficient cavity pressure or fail to remove the air in the cavity
that makes barriers, so that the melt flow cannot fully contact the surface of the mold, or the
cooling rate of the material is reduced that let the product surface is severely dented. The
shrinkage cavity is mostly located on the reinforcement surface and gate.
Prevention:
In terms of product design, It’s necessary to prevent uneven wall thickness caused by
reinforcement. Generally, the reinforcement thickness is 50% of the wall thickness, so that the
wall thickness at the corners of the reinforcement is uniform.
When selecting materials, it is important to choose materials with low shrinkage rates. In
terms of mold, set runner at area with wall thickness. In terms of injection molding process,
reduced the mold temperature and melt temperature, increased the injection pressure, pressure
holding time and injection rate, strengthened the cooling for the places prone to shrinkage
cavity, increased the section size of gate, increased the pressure transmission effect, and
prevented shrinkage cavity at the gate.
b. Matt, Blushing, rub mark and wrinkle
Causes:
These defects are mostly caused by mold temperature is too low, polymer melt temperature
is too high, and cooling speed is too fast. While the melt is still under filling, a very hard shell is
already formed on the cavity wall. With the various forces, the shell turns blushing and muddy. In
serious cases, the shell may be torn and displaced, resulting in rubbing marks or wrinkles. For
example, when injecting thin products of low-density polyethylene with low melt index, this
defect is easy to occur. The melt enters the small area of mold cavity and quickly form a thin

18

 Operation Manual

shell, and the thin layer is subject to great stress to tear the surface before the mold filling is
completed. The other reason for this type of defect is the irregular pulsating flow of the melt in
the mold, such as when the polymer melt small jets are injected into the mold cavity when the
gate size is small and the injection speed is fast, and small jets are cooled down for a period of
time and then fused with the subsequent melt.
Prevention:
In terms of materials, choose materials with good gloss and density. High impact
polystyrene and ABS have low luster, and materials with poor thermal conductivity have high
luster; Selecting carbon tool steel for mold can easily produce luster. When making mold cavity,
it’s necessary to carefully grind (1 µ m carborundum) mold cavity surface. For injection molding
process, properly increase mold temperature and use local heating at the mold gate.
c.

Silver streak and peeling layer

Causes:
During mold filling, volatile gases are separated out from the wave front peak. These gases
are decomposed by heating of the material, and the gas is distributed on the surface of the
product that causes a silver streak. These silver streaks often look like a "V" with the tip facing
the gate. When the moisture content is too high, the heating will produce steam. During
plasticizing, the air entrained by the material cannot be removed due to the unfavorable
operation of the screw, and the silver streak will occur. In some cases, large bubble is stretched
into flat bubble that stays the surface of the product, so that the surface of the product is
peeling. Sometimes, silver streaks are produced because the temperature gradient from the
barrel to the nozzle is too high for the shear cutter.
Prevention:
For materials, selecting materials with low hygroscopicity or use better drying equipment to
fully dry the materials and reduce the moisture content to the lowest value. In terms of process,
reduced the melt temperature, increased the mold temperature, stabilized the nozzle
temperature, increased the back pressure during plasticization, and selected the screw with
larger compression ratio. In terms of the mold, an exhaust slot is provided so that the gas can be
easily discharged from the mold cavity when the mold is filled.
d. Gas mark, dark grain and dark spot
Causes:
The appearance of dark grain and dark spot is mostly caused by the overheating
decomposition of materials, and some are caused by the uneven plasticization that leaves
un-melted particles on the product which will look like dark spots from the appearance.
Sometimes, it is caused by foreign object in the material, dust from the mold, hopper feeder or
barrel corrosion plating drop-off. During mold filling, the air in the mold is compressed, the
temperature rises and the burned polymer chars and such defects often occur at the fusion
seams.
19

Operation Manual

Prevention:
In terms of process, let polymer granules are fully dried, the melt temperature is reduced
and the back pressure is increased. In terms of mold, set up the exhaust slots. For the screw, use
larger compression rate。
e. Warpage
Causes:
Various thermoplastic polymers have varying degrees of warpage. The warpage of
crystalline polymer is larger than that of amorphous polymer, because the number of molecules
oriented in the flow direction is larger than the number of molecules oriented perpendicular to
the flow direction. Therefore, the shrinkage caused by relaxation perpendicular to the flow
direction is smaller than that in the flow direction. This shrinkage difference causes uneven
internal stress, which is the main factor for warpage. If there is additive in the polymer, it will
increase the warpage degree of the product. For example, the color pigment can increase the
shrinkage of high density polyethylene, and cause severe warpage of the product. Such warpage
is mostly caused by orientation. Uneven cooling of the product in the mold also causes warpage,
which is mostly caused by temperature stress.
As shown in the figure below, the product warpage with different temperature of two sides
during injection molding:

Hot

Cold

Hot

(1) Flat

Hot

Cold

(2) Hot box and mold (3) Cold box and mold

Prevention:
In terms of process, consider reducing the orientation can reduce the warpage, so it’s
necessary to quickly fill the mold to shorten the heat transfer time of the melt and use sufficient
materials to compensate for the heat shrinkage. Increase the injection pressure and holding time
can minimize the warping, but be aware that too high material temperature can also cause
warping. In terms of materials, the narrower the molecular weight distribution of polymer is, the
less the warping is. Crystalline polymer products are easy to shrink or warping, products with
high crystallinity and density tend to reduce the warping tendency. For the mold, the uneven
temperature control at the cooling area will lead the orientation and the crystallinity of the part

20

 Operation Manual

is different, resulting in uneven internal stress and warping. Uneven release force or improper
ejection area will cause warpage. Since the gate is too small to fill the mold cavity with material
before closing the gate, it is easy to cause warpage. Opening the gate at the thick section is
beneficial for the material to completely fill the mold cavity; otherwise, the gate opening at the
thin section will cause high internal stress and cause warpage.
In general, the conditions for reducing warpage are: reducing orientation, increasing gate
size, appropriately lowering the melt mold temperature, accelerating injection speed, increasing
injection pressure, prolonging injection and pressure holding time, reducing gate pressure,
increasing wall thickness, and increasing reinforcement at rib and fillet. In addition, for the
material, adding about 0.1% of the stearic acid for demolding. For molds, design sufficient draft
angle, change the large gate to a small gate or set multiple gates to reduce the pressure and
molecular orientation near the gate, grind the mold well, and improve the surface smoothness of
the mold cavity or making the force of the ejection screw balanced to reduce products warping.
f.

Crazing

Causes:
Crazing and crackles are different in appearance. Crazing is without gaps which arranged
along the force direction of molecular chains under the action of stress, but can be recovered
after annealing. When the ejection force of the demolding is unbalanced, or the demolding
cavity causes a large vacuum suction, crazing will occur. In addition, when the product is painted,
the effect of the solvent in the coating will cause crazing on the product surface with residual
stress.
Prevention:
In terms of process, adopting favorable methods to eliminate internal stress, such as
increasing the melt and mold temperature can keep the melt in a good flow state and reduce the
injection pressure. Adding an annealing process to the whole process can eliminate crazing, but
this may increase energy consumption.
g. Welding Line (Water line)
Causes:
As previously mentioned, this occurs when more than two gates produced two streams of
melt flow and closed again (holes and pins), resulting in two wavefront peaks being blocked by
foreign matter (e.g. gas or inclusions). In general, the strength of the welding line is not small,
but due to inclusions in the welding line and large internal stress, the strength becomes small;
especially composite materials, due to uneven composition, it will become weaker.

21

 Operation Manual

Prevention:
In terms of process, reduce the forming conditions of welding line. Such as appropriately
increase the mold and melt temperature, and increase injection pressure and speed. For the
mold, set up the exhaust slot to facilitate the exhaust of gas.
Sometimes, a "tab" is attached to the welding line to make the welding line occur on the
tab and then cut it off, as shown in the figure:

h. Flash (burr)
Causes:
Flash is formed when the melt flow overflows from the parting surface of the mold during
mold filling and solidifies after cooling. There are so many reasons: in terms of equipment, the
clamping force of injection molding machine is too small or the system rigidity is poor. In the
aspect of mold, it is caused by reasons such as improper material application, unreasonable
structure, insufficient rigidity or low geometric accuracy, and low processing accuracy and
assembly accuracy, which make the moving and fixed molds unable to be flattened or closed
tightly.
Prevention:
For equipment, select an injection molding machine with good rigidity of clamping
mechanism and clamping force that meets the standard. It’s better to choose the injection
molding machine with multi-stage injection or feedback control system.
In terms of polymer materials, select materials with higher melt viscosity. Nylon with low
viscosity is easy to form flash.
In terms of process, adjust sufficient clamping force, and appropriately reduced melt
temperature, mold temperature and injection speed. Adopt Multi-stage pressure, multi-stage
speed and feedback control technologies. Ensure accurate switching of "seal pressure".
Flash occurs during severe mold expansion. For some products, even if there is no flash, the
dimensional accuracy of the product will be out of tolerance due to mold expansion, so flash
should be prevented.
i.

Change in product thickness

22

 Operation Manual

Causes:
This thickness change refers to the change in product size when the cavity dimensions
accuracy is correct. It’s caused by insufficient rigidity of the mold and core, and deformation due
to the filling pressure during injection. For example, when the mold cavity pressure is 49Mpa
(500kgf/cm2), the mold cavity will have a large elastic deformation, and when the pressure is
removed, the deformation will recover again.
Prevention:
Select materials with good rigidity and a hardness of HRc52 or higher after processing. In
terms of mold, the precision should be high and the joint of each part should be accurate.
It is worth noting that the surface quality of injection molding products also affects the
secondary processing, such as coating, printing, heat printing, vacuum gold plating,
electroplating, etc.

2.3 Safety of commissioning
(1) Do not restart the motor before it has completely stopped, and avoid repeating to start
the motor within a short time (generally about 30 seconds) to avoid damage to the
motor and contact switch
(2) When any safety door is not closed properly, the motor will not operate or operate any
action
(3) When installing the mold or the operator must enter the upper and lower mold plate,
please confirm that the motor stops running
(4) Please try to ensure the machine operator and die installation personnel are the same
person.
(5) Do not apply the oil on the screw, screw barrel, electric heating plate and other injection
related parts.
(6) Cooling water should be used properly. The machine should use proper cooling water to
cool the hydraulic oil and die. The water pipe should be connected to the inlet and outlet
of the heat exchanger. The inlet water temperature should be lower than 30 ℃. In order
to prevent the oil temperature higher than 55 ℃, sufficient water supply should be
ensured. The cooling water should be soft water without mineral impurities. Well water
or other water sources contain many impurities and mineral components, which are easy
to produce scaling and not recommend.
(7) Self-lubricating bearing is adopted for this machine, so do not apply oil on the machine
tie bar.
(8) Please select the most appropriate person to replace the mold, and pay attention to the
"Special Note" in this manual.
(9) Regulate the back pressure of melt: the plastic injection molding process should properly
consider the time of plastic melting, that is, the plastic should be evenly melted in the
shortest time. Therefore, it is necessary to master the appropriate speed of the melt
(material storage) and improve the productivity by guaranteeing the product quality. The
speed of melt (material storage) is achieved by controlling the rotating speed of melt and
the retreat speed of melt (material storage) screw. The former can be preset on the
panel of the control box by touching the button in advance, and the latter can be realized
by adjusting the melt back pressure valve. When the screw rotates, the plastic is pushed
to the head of the screw, and the screw moves backward. If there is resistance restricting
its retreat when the screw moves backward, the corresponding melt is pushed to the
head of the screw slowly, and the melt is dense. Therefore, the speed and uniformity of
plastic melting can also be controlled through the adjustment of the melt (material
storage) back pressure valve to meet the requirements of injection molding process.

3. Operation Safety
3.1 Safety in machine operation
(1) When the hydraulic pump stop working for the injection molding machine, firstly,
confirm whether the upper and lower mold plates are combined, and keep the mold
closed without pressure. When working inside the injection molding machine, confirm
that the motor switch is turned off and the main power switch is turned off first.
(2) Do not remove the safety cover or safety door to avoid serious injury to human body.
(3) When the electric heating switch is turned on for heating, the temperature around the
screw barrel is very high, and the interior of the heating element is energized by high
voltage. Be careful not to touch the screw barrel!
(4) When the hydraulic motor is running, keep your body away from any movable
component. Keep your hands away from the running injection molding machine. In order
to avoid serious injury to the operator's hands, do not place the bar or hands in the
clamping unit, turntable, injection unit and injection nozzle. When the injection screw is
rotating, do not put the bar or hand into the melting cylinder.
(5) Do not put hands on the running feeder.
(6) Do not climb on the machine when it is running. The top end of the molding machine is
quite high. When climbing the top end of the molding machine for maintenance, be very
careful and avoid slipping or falling.
(7) When the injection molding machine is operating in the injection operation, no matter
what kind of operation mode the injection machine is in, no one is allowed to open the
safety door and enter inside the safety door.
(8) During injection, the human body must be kept away from the injection unit. When the
molding machine is in operation, do not place hands on any movable component,
injection screw barrel, nozzle, and etc.
(9) In any case, do not put your hands into any part of the molding machine through the
upper and lower side of the safety door.
(10) When the hydraulic oil temperature rises and exceeds 60 ℃, please shut down the
machine immediately and check the cause. Sometimes, the rise of hydraulic oil
temperature will damage the water pipe inside the hydraulic cooler or overload the
motor.
(11) Personnel are protected by emergency switch, safety door and screw barrel cover when
operating the machine. Therefore, these protective devices cannot be removed in any
way.
(12) Do not operate the machine before the safety door or safety baffle is closed stably. A
suitable micro-switch is installed on the safety door. Confirm safety devices are
functioning every day. All safety doors must be tightly closed, or the machine cannot
operate. When any safety door is not closed, the micro-switch on the safety door will
41

Operation Manual

actuate to make the machine unable to operate. Therefore, the safety door must be
closed during the operation of the machine.
(13) Based on the ergonomic principle, the height from the operation panel to the ground is
600MM-1600MM. When the height of the operation panel of the machine is higher than
1600MM, the working platform must be added for operation to reach the proper height.

the emergency stop button is reset, please switch the button in the
direction of the arrow of the emergency stop button.
3.6 Shutdown at the end of work
(1) Stop moving. Switch the operation mode from automatic to semi-automatic, and the
injection molding machine stops after 1 injection molding cycle. Close the baffle of the
hopper and stop charging. Close mold and withdraw injection device.
(2) Discharge resin (clean). Perform a cleaning action to drain the remaining resin from the
injection cylinder.
(3) Cut off the power supply of heater.
(4) Remove the mold. Remove the mold after ensuring that sliding plates do not move.
(5) Cut off the motor power supply. Press the [MOTOR OFF] button on the control panel.
The indicator is off.
(6) Cut off the operating power supply.
(7) Cut off the main switch. Switch the air switch on the main power box side of the
injection molding machine side to [OFF].
(8) Stop water supply. Confirm that the temperature of each heater drops to around normal
temperature, and then stop supplying cooling water. If the cooling water supply is
stopped while the heater is in the high temperature, the components of injection units
can be damaged. Be sure to stop supplying cooling water after the temperature of heater
drops to normal temperature.
(9) Remove excess material and remove hopper when the machine is shutdown.

Chapter VII Maintenance
Injection molding machine production generally operates 24 hours a day(shift system), and
generally will not shutdown except for order reduction or public holidays. For the machine in
working state for a long time, we must pay more attention in maintenance, and try to find out
and solve problems before the machine malfunctions. Otherwise, once the machine breaks down,
it must be stopped for maintenance, which will seriously affect the production and delay the
delivery date. Therefore, it is very important to maintain the injection molding machine.
To do a good job in the maintenance of injection molding machine, the maintenance
contents must be classified according to the possible failure frequency: list the contents with the
highest frequency into daily maintenance, list the contents with lower frequency into weekly
maintenance, then classify the monthly maintenance and annual maintenance contents.
It is better to arrange specially-assigned person to take charge of the determined
maintenance contents, so as to ensure that the determined maintenance work is effectively
implemented according to the planned arrangement. The necessary records must be made at the
end of each maintenance work, so as to provide basis for the evaluation of the machine in the
future work. For the hydraulic injection molding machine, the maintenance is mainly carried out
from the following aspects.
(1) Maintenance of electrical system
a. When checking the high-voltage components in the machine, do not turn on the main
power supply unless necessary.
b. When changing the mold, do not let the cooling water flow into the control box.
c.

Check whether the temperature in the control box is too high, that may affect the
normal operation of the electronic board.

d. When replacing the relay, the relay with specified voltage should be used.
(2) Maintenance of hydraulic system. The pressure oil temperature of the injection molding
machine should be kept between 30 ° C and 50 ° C. If the oil temperature exceeds 60 ° C, the
following problems will occur:
a. Deterioration of pressure oil due to oxidation.
b. The reduced viscosity of pressure oil causes damage to oil pump, oil leakage, pressure
drop and reduced working efficiency.
c.

Accelerate the aging rate of oil seal.

d. Prevent the cooling water from leaking into the oil cylinder, and pay special attention to
check whether there is water leakage inside the cooler. Disassemble and clean the cooler
every 6 months.
e. The hydraulic oil of the injection molding machine must be replaced every 3000-4000
hours. When replacing the hydraulic oil, do not mix the old and new oil together. At the
same time, twist down the oil screen in the oil cylinder for cleaning.
49

 Operation Manual

f.

The oil valve fails because the valve core is blocked by foreign matters. Remove the valve
core from the oil valve and clean it with diesel or kerosene (or immersed in clean
pressure oil), and then remove the foreign matters with compressed air. Do not
disassemble the oil valve unless it is confirmed that the oil valve is blocked by foreign
matters and causes malfunction of the injection molding machine.

g. Cleaning and maintenance of oil suction strainer. It is installed at the inlet of the oil
pressure pump, and the pipeline is buried in the oil tank. Its function is to filter. The filter
screen should not be damaged during disassembly. The machine should be cleaned and
maintained monthly for the first three months, and cleaned and maintained every three
months in the following year. If there is little dirt and debris, the maintenance interval
should be properly adjusted and extended. Gently remove and clean the dirt and
sundries accumulated on the filter screen with diesel. Completely clean the attachments
from the inside (center) to the outside of the filter screen with an air gun, and pay
attention not to damage the filter screen. When installing, make sure that the fixing
device is at the inlet pipeline of the oil pressure pump.

d. Be sure to stop the motor when installing or removing the oil filter screen.
e. After installing the oil filter screen, start the motor to test the oil pressure and

(3) Maintenance of mold clamping part
a. The service life of mold clamping parts is very long, but each moving part should be
properly lubricated, otherwise the service life will be reduced due to wear.
b. Keep the sliding feet of the moving mold plate and four tie bars clean and lubricated.
c.

Avoid mold clamping by using pressure that are close to or exceed maximum system

d. Special fast mold clamping speed cannot be used during mold adjustment.
e. Control the most appropriate position of the mould clamping stroke to reduce the
impact on the machine when mould unloading and clamping.
f.

Avoid using too large and too heavy moulds.

(4) Inspection and maintenance of hydraulic hose
a. Ensure that the fluid temperature and ambient temperature during operation do not
exceed the allowable temperature range of the hose. Ensure that hoses and connectors
are compatible with the environment or are effectively protected. Environmental
conditions include ultraviolet light, heat, ozone, moisture, water, saline water, chemicals,
contaminated air, and etc., which may cause degradation and early failure.
b. Hoses must be carefully checked before installation. Check all components for correct
form, size, length, etc. Check the cleanliness of the hose; check the inner bore for
blockage, blisters, wrinkles, etc.
c.

Hoses and connectors must be properly installed so that no distortion or torque is
transmitted to the hose. Do not use the head pulled from the hose.

d. Air must be exhausted after all hoses are installed. The system operates under the
maximum working pressure of the system. The system acts correctly and functions
normally. There is no leakage at each connection. Personnel must be kept away from the
hazardous area during the test.
e. The hose must be replaced immediately and checked at least once a month in case of
the following conditions:
The connector slides in the hose
Damage, cuts and abrasion of outer rubber layer (exposure of any reinforcement layer)
Hose is hardened, cracked, and burned
Crack, damage, serious corrosion on joint
Leakage of connector or hose
Hose winding, crushing, flattening, and twisting
Hose outer blistering, softening, aging, and loosening
f.

The following items must be tightened or repaired or replaced as required, and checked
at least once a month:
Oil leakage at engine block oil port
Blockage of dirt
Pipe clamp, sheath and protector
System oil level, fluid type, air entrapment

g. The replacement period must be determined by the previous service life, national or
industrial standards or the degree of damage caused to the failure. Theoretically, the
hose should be replaced once a year.
h. Explosion-proof (release) chains must be installed at both ends of the interface with

pressure over 5MPa.
(5) Maintenance of injection part
a. Keep the injection unit tie bars lubricated and clean.
b. Keep the injection unit surface clean and dry.
c.

Except for plastics, pigments and additives do not put anything into the hopper. If return
materials are used, the hopper magnet must be put into the hopper to prevent metal
fragments from entering the melt cylinder.

d. When the melt cylinder does not reach the preset temperature, do not start the melt
motor or use the reverse cable (loosening) to avoid damaging the transmission system
components.
e. Before using special plastics, consult the plastic supplier to determine which injection
screw is suitable for this kind of plastics.
f.

Use the relevant methods provided by the plastic supplier to correctly replace the plastic
and clean the melt cylinder.

g. Regularly check each part of the injection unit and tighten the loose parts to ensure that
the two injection cylinders are installed in balance, so as to avoid damage to the oil seal
of the injection cylinder and abrasion of the oil leakage pump core.
h. Regularly drain the lubricating grease of the hydraulic motor and replace it with new
grease.
i.

When the melt temperature is normal and there is continuous black spot or
discoloration of the melt, check whether the rubber ring and the rubber gasket of the
injection screw are damaged.

1. General Principles for Machine Maintenance
(1) To remove or move the nozzle, firstly reduce the pressure inside the screw barrel since
the temperature sometimes causes the plastic in the closed screw barrel to react and
create explosive pressure in the screw barrel. Reduce the temperature of the screw
barrel and the internal pressure, and press the release button to make the injection
screw backward.

(2) It is quite dangerous to heat the plastic in the screw barrel for too long, because
long-time heating of the plastic will generate toxic gas and eject it through the nozzle.
(3) When foreign matters enter the nozzle, do not increase the temperature or pressure to
force the objects out. The power must be switched off and the nozzle must be removed
before removing the foreign matter.
(4) Take out the hard objects carefully when they fall into the feeding hopper.
(5) Make sure to turn off the main power switch before removing the cover of the screw
barrel.
(6) The main power switch must be turned off during the maintenance of the electrical box,

also should be measured under the limit of the machine.
(7) For sliding parts and rotary table lubricating oil, only synthetic oil approved by USFDA,

(8) It is forbidden to apply lubricating oil on the injection screw, screw barrel and electric
heating plate.
(9) All movable parts of the machine should be lubricated with a small amount of oil without
grease.
(10) When installing or maintaining the machine, installing molds and installing automation,
two or more persons must operate to avoid hazards due to movement.
(11) Check whether the nuts on the mold plate of the molding machine and the nuts of other
devices are tightened. Please refer to the maintenance and inspection table and make
sure to conduct regular maintenance and inspection.
(12) Check all liquid equipment and devices, such as oil circuit, oil pipe, oil groove, solenoid
valve, pressure controller, oil pipe head and cooler. Please refer to the maintenance and
inspection table and make sure to conduct regular maintenance and inspection.
(13) Replace the hydraulic oil of the molding machine regularly, and apply lubricating oil and
proper temperature oil to the surrounding machines.
(14) When replacing the hydraulic oil pipe and connectors, repair and replace immediately
once any abnormalities found.
(15) Tie bar, nut and screw must be checked regularly. If there is any loosened, please lock
them tightly.
(16) Electric leakage may cause shock or electric shock. Check electrical circuit and electric
leakage regularly. In case of any electric leakage, shut down the machine immediately
and turn off the power supply before repair.
(17) Incorrect inspection, maintenance or repair may cause personal injury and equipment
damage. Regularly check the machine, mould, surrounding machinery, wire, hydraulic
motor, and etc. In case of any abnormal condition, please shut down the machine
immediately and cut off the power supply of the surrounding machine before repair.

4. Instructions for Lubrication and Cooling of Machines
(1) Lubrication of machines
Once the injection molding machine is in production, it will operate repeatedly in
the same position. Therefore, if the movable parts are worn, the service life of the
machine will be significantly shortened. Lubricate and add lubricating oil/grease
according to the working condition of the machine. When filling grease, be sure to cut
off the power supply of the molding machine and stop the molding machine before
operation. The lubricating oil or grease specified by MULTITECH should be used. The use
of lubricants or grease of a brand other than that specified by MULTITECH may damage
some parts of the machine. Only a few following parts of the machine need to be added
with lubricating grease, so there is no lubricating system. Clean weekly during operation
and inject grease with grease gun every 500 hours. The grease fitting parts include: 1
mold sliding part (sliding table or turntable) 2 tie bar moving part 3 injecting
transmission shaft parts.
(2) Cooling of machines
a. Cooling of hydraulic oil: the temperature of hydraulic oil is generally about 40℃. If
the temperature is too high, the oil viscosity will be reduced and the oil seal will be
aged. When the oil temperature exceeds 60℃ or the oil level is lower than the oil
inlet pipe, the motor will automatically shut down and display an error signal to
avoid damage due to overheating. The hydraulic oil in the machine is cooled by the
cooler. In order to prolong the service life of the injection molding machine cooler
and maintain and prevent the reduction of efficiency, it is generally recommended to
use the cooling water equivalent to tap water. Connect the water pipe to the inlet
and outlet of the cooler. The inlet water temperature is lower than 30℃. In order to
prevent the oil temperature from being higher than 50℃, the water supply shall be
at least 65. P. M above.
b. Check all connections are tight before starting the cooler, and confirm the water
57

Operation Manual

supply and drainage valves of the cooler are open.

c.

When starting the cooler, do not open the water inlet valve quickly. As a large
amount of cooling water flows through the cooler, a layer of ‘supercooled layer’ with
poor thermal conductivity will be formed on the surface of the heat exchange tube.
Even if the water volume is large later, the best cooling effect will not be achieved.

d. If galvanic corrosion occurs at the water side, galvanic zinc bar can be installed at the
specified position (reserved hole) of the water inlet and outlet cover.
e. In order to improve the heat exchange performance and prevent scale formation,
the cooling water temperature should be set as low as possible and the water flow
rate should be larger.
f.

The oil side pressure of the cooler should be greater than the water side pressure.

g. In cold seasons and when the cooler is not operating, the water must be drained to
prevent freezing and cracking. The temperature of cooling water should be lower
than 28 ℃, and the water pressure should exceed 0.1Mpa (below 0.4Mpa). Avoid
using hard water that may block the cooling water pipe.
h. In winter, the low temperature of cooling water may cause the working oil
temperature to be too low, which affects injection molding. The amount of cooling
water should be adjusted according to different seasons. If the cooling water is likely
to freeze, close the water supply valve and open the drain plug to drain water after
the equipment has finished running.
i.

The machine is not installed a shut-off valve at the drain. If overhead piping is
installed in the factory, a shut-off valve should be installed at the drain to prevent
backflow that maybe caused by pressure differences in the pipeline, and to facilitate
maintenance and inspection of oil coolers, etc.

5. Replacement of Hydraulic Oil and Coolant of Machines
(1) Proper use and maintenance of hydraulic oil
The quality of hydraulic oil has a great impact on the performance of the hydraulic
system, and many faults are caused by it. Therefore, preventing the oil pollution is an
important part for the use and maintenance of the hydraulic system.
a. Causes of impurities in oil, hazards to the system, and preventive measures;
According to the relevant data, more than 70% faults of the hydraulic device are
58

 Operation Manual

caused by impurities in the oil, so the oil must be kept clean. In order to prevent the
oil from being polluted, the pipeline must be cleaned before installation, and each
component must be cleaned with diesel or kerosene before assembly. However, it is
prohibited to wipe with fabric with fiber to prevent impurities. All seals must be in
good conditions, and the use of non-oil resistant seals or hoses is prohibited. It’s
better to clean the hydraulic system after the final assembly is completed.
b. Causes of air generation in oil, hazards to system, and prevention measures;
The main reasons for air entering the hydraulic system:
[1] Air is directly mixed into the oil.
[2] The air dissolved in the oil is separated and enters the hydraulic system.
[3] Poor oil quality, poor defoaming performance, etc.
The air entering the system will cause the following main hazards:
[1] Increased the elasticity of the oil and reduced the rigidity of the hydraulic
system.
[2] Because the compressibility of low-pressure air is thousands of times that of
oil, even a small amount of air mixed in the system can cause the system to
lag and lose stability.
[3] When the air mixed in the oil is suddenly compressed, local high
temperature will be generated to evaporate and oxidize the oil, and the oil
will deteriorate and turn black. The oil will be contaminated.
[4] Disrupting the continuity of liquid flow and generates "air plug" in the
small-diameter pipeline, making the valve malfunction.
[5] Cause irregular flow of liquid and hydraulic shock.
[6] The diffusion of air bubbles in the oil reduces the lubricating performance,
deteriorates the chemical stability, and accelerates the oxidation of oil and
the corrosion of parts.
To prevent air from entering the oil, the following measures are recommended:
[1] Both the suction pipe and the oil return pipe must be extended below the
oil level and kept at a sufficient depth.
[2] Each part of the system should always be filled with oil. A one-way valve can
be installed at the pump outlet and a back pressure valve can be installed
on the oil return circuit.
[3] Maintain good sealing performance of the system and replace faulty seals in
time.
[4] Install the exhaust valve at the highest place of the system (generally on the
hydraulic cylinder) for exhaust.
c.

Causes of water in oil, hazards to the system, and preventive measures;
The main reasons for mixing water: leakage of cooling water from the oil tank
cover, leakage of cooler or heat exchanger, and air with high humidity entering the
59

 Operation Manual

oil tank.
The main method to prevent the oil from mixing with water is to strictly prevent
cooling water from entering the oil tank cover and replace damaged water coolers
and heat exchangers in time. If there is too much water, remove the water by taking
effective measures or replace with new oil.
d. Hazards of high oil temperature to the system and preventive measures;
Generally, it is appropriate to keep the oil temperature of hydraulic machinery
within 30-50 ℃. The rise of oil temperature will reduce the viscosity of the oil, thus
increasing the leakage, reducing the efficiency of the pump, and slowing down the
movement speed of the actuator assembly, affecting the stability of the work. When
the viscosity is reduced, the oil will become thin, and the lubricating film on the
relative moving surface will become thin or even lose efficacy, resulting in increased
mechanical wear. The rise of oil temperature will accelerate the oxidation of oil,
cause deterioration of oil and reduce the service life of oil. In addition, the rise of oil
temperature will cause the thermal expansion of valve components, reduce the fit
interval, increase the wear, cause the valve core to be stuck, rapid aging, and
deteriorate the sealing elements and lose the sealing performance.
In order to prevent the oil temperature from being too high, pay attention to
the following issues:
[1] Select the correct viscosity of the oil. Both high and low viscosity is not
conducive to hydraulic transmission. High viscosity will increase the energy
consumption during oil flows. Low viscosity will increase the leakage, so
both of them will lead the oil temperature increase.
[2] Maintain sufficient oil level in oil tank, and select the cooler with sufficient
cooling capacity to ensure sufficient circulating cooling conditions for the oil
in the system.
[3] Ensuring the hydraulic equipment is in normal operation, the pump pressure
and back pressure should be adjusted as low as possible to reduce energy
consumption
[4] Keep the equipment clean, and timely remove the dirt on the oil tank and oil
pipe for heat dissipation.
(2) Replacement of hydraulic oil
The oil inside the hydraulic unit will gradually deteriorate during operating. However,
it is not easy to replace the oil due to inability to find a time to stop the machine to
replace the new oil, and the new oil is also quite expensive. Once the oil deteriorates
during operation, it will proceed rapidly, resulting in a shortened operating life of the
hydraulic unit. Therefore, in order to confirm the deterioration state of the operating oil,
the maintenance safety log in operation must be recorded and should be checked
regularly. The methods for determining whether the operating oil has deteriorated
60

Operation Manual

include visual inspection of the appearance and analysis of the characteristics of the
action to understand the situation. Both are comparing with new oil. The appearance
method is to take out the oil from the operating oil tank, and then take out the oil from
the tank 5cm above the bottom when the unit is in operation for 24 hours, and compare
it with the new oil in a test tube. The chart shows below:

lubricating property. If there are white bubbles in the oil tank, it indicates that air is
mixed, and the pressure will be unstable. Refer to the motor start test for exhaust
methods
The performance test of operating oil can be entrusted to the oil supplier.
When replacing the hydraulic oil, firstly drain all the operating oil and the oil in the
hydraulic cylinder, and clean the inner wall of the oil tank with kerosene, because the
original oil will deteriorate the new oil. When refueling the oil tank, open the upper
cover of the oil filling port to allow the oil to pass through the filter screen for refueling.
The oil level in the oil tank must be above the centerline of the level gauge and not
exceed the upper edge of the coupling between the motor and the oil pump. Generally,
the hydraulic oil should be replaced after 3000 to 4000 hours of use.

Note: Once the hydraulic oil or oil pump is replaced, refill the pump chamber with oil
before starting.
(3) Replacement of oil filter screen
The oil filter screen is used to filter the dust on the suction side of the pump,
generally 80 meshes. If the filter screen is blocked by paint or dust, the filtration area will
be insufficient, and the vacuum vortex is easy to occur, so it needs to be cleaned. When
cleaning, first turn off the oil pump, open the oil filler, remove the filter screen, and clean

fuel tank to prevent dust from being sucked into the bottom of the tank and causing
mesh blockage. If set near the oil level, it will suck in air. Generally, the filter screen
should be cleaned at least once a year.

should be carried out every 5-10 months.

1.2 Precautions for Maintenance and Inspection of Solenoid Valve
The hydraulic circuit is equipped with various solenoid valves for switching action.
When the controller output voltage drives the solenoid valve to actuate, the solenoid
valve should be lit, and the voltage at the solenoid valve coil end should be 220VAC (or
24VDC for DC valve).
When the solenoid valve lights up and is magnetizing, but the solenoid valve does
not operate, the solenoid valve may be blocked by foreign matters and cannot operate or
operate improperly. At this time, 3mm or 4mm hexagon wrench can be used to push the
"manual operation action tip" on the side of the solenoid valve. If it still doesn’t work,
the solenoid valve must be removed and cleaned.
When the solenoid valve lights up but is not magnetizing:
(1) Confirm the voltage of solenoid valve terminal is within the above scope of use.
(2) Solenoid valve coil failure or burnout.
When the injection molding machine is used for a long time, the oil seal used on the
mating surface of the solenoid valve will harden, which may cause oil leakage. Check the
oil leakage of solenoid valve every 6 months and replace the oil seal as required.

1.6 Examples of faults caused by hydraulic actuation oil pollution
Dirt in the oil tank, piping and from outside of the oil pressure unit will continue
circulate in the oil circuit. While cycling and crushing into smaller particles, it also
increases the wear of the hydraulic machine. This dirt accumulates in every corner of the
hydraulic machine, causing various negative impacts.

78

1.7 Prevention methods for faults caused by hydraulic actuation oil pollution
Before preventing malfunctions of the hydraulic machine caused by dirt, the most
important thing is to keep the oil tank clean.
a. Do not leave any residue…... clean well.
Do not leave dirt in the oil tank, oil circuit board and piping , and keep them
clean, especially in the oil tank.
b. Do not let in...... use good oil filters when supplying oil.
When adding new oil, be sure to use the oil filter and the oil filler mesh.
However, the mesh of the filter screen at the oil filler is too rough and is likely
no filtering, so it is necessary to use a 5-10 micro kilometer (μ m) oil filter for
refueling. Because the air often flows into the oil tank, an air filter with a
density of 5-10 μ m should also be used.
c.

Do not let it (dirt) circulate...... cut off its circulation with the return oil filter.
Because the suction port of the oil pump cannot use a fine mesh filter, the dirt
in the oil tank is also sucked in and circulates in the hydraulic circuit. Although
pipeline oil filters are effective in filtering, the filtration rate decreases as dirt
gradually crushes and becomes finer. The most effective way is to use a return
oil filter, as dirt will flow back to the tank and be filtered before returning to the
tank. Density of 3-5μm return oil filter screen is the most effective one.
Although the return oil filter may be blocked due to filtering dirt in a short
period of time during use, if it can be cleaned once (mostly will replace the
filter), the frequency of blocking will be extremely reduced. This is because the
circulation of dirt is blocked, which has the function of inhibiting the multiply of
dirt.

d. Maintaining, Upward…... Active use of machines outside the unit.
For ultra-small dirt that cannot be removed by oil filter, external filter,
electrostatic oil purifier and permanent magnet should be used. As the limited
processing capacity of these equipment, it is necessary to use a
high-performance return oil filter at the same time. (If there is no good return
oil filter, more dirt will multiply during circulation than will be filtered out.)
When using permanent magnets, it is necessary to know the method of
removing dirt attached to the magnet. Dirt attached to the magnet is also one
of the reasons for the deterioration of the actuating oil.
e. Pay attention to the dead corners in the circuit...... Clean the inside of the oil
cylinder.
The dirt on the puncher of the hydraulic cylinder will be guided into the interior
of the hydraulic cylinder, contaminating the oil inside the hydraulic cylinder.
Sometimes the piping is too long, so the actuating oil flowing into the oil
pressure cannot be replaced, causing dirt to accumulate in one side. This dirt
80

 Operation Manual

will slowly multiply, and the solenoid valve will also be affected. As a result, no
matter how much cleaning is done inside the oil tank, its effectiveness cannot
be achieved.
Countermeasures: Install a bypass circuit and stop valve near the hydraulic
cylinder. During regular maintenance, first push the hydraulic cylinder to one
side without releasing the button, then open the stop valve, and then push the
other side. Repeat the action to filter the oil in the hydraulic cylinder.

3. Safety Precautions during Machine Repairing
(1) The person responsible for major repairs and inspections should have a good
understanding of the characteristics of melted resin and is familiar with the structure,
assembly and use essentials of the machine. If the danger cannot be accurately
predicted and handled correctly, a major accident may occur.
(2) The maintenance and inspection of the electrical system should be carried out by
qualified electrical maintenance personnel recognized by the factory.
(3) Please strictly follow the warnings and precautions listed in the operation manual.
(4) Equipped with accumulators on injection molding machines. Even if the main power
supply of the injection molding machine is cut off and the pump and motor are stopped,
high-pressure hydraulic oil will still exist in the hydraulic circuit. In any maintenance and
inspection of hydraulic machines, please be sure to open the manual pressure release
valve of accumulators before starting, and confirm that there is no residual high-pressure
hydraulic oil.
(5) During maintenance, please hang crosspieces and ropes around to indicate that
maintenance work is in progress.

82

 Operation Manual

(6) During maintenance, please hang the crosspieces and ropes that prohibit power input to
indicate that power should not be turned on
(7) During maintenance, appropriate work clothes and protective equipment (glasses, gloves,
and safety helmets) should be worn.
(8) Do not touch or operate electrical wiring, components, and switches with wet or greasy
hands to avoid electric shock.
(9) Do not change the settings and operating parameters of components. If it is necessary to
make changes, please contact us.
(10) Do not modify any components without the permission of our company, as this may
result in unpredictable actions and major accidents.
(11) Prepare the parts for maintenance in advance.
(12) When replacing parts and components, only products designated by our company can be
used.
(13) When the body enters between the binder plates, the operating power, pump, and
motor should be cut off.
(14) When working at heights, a safe workbench or ladder should be used.
(15) When dismantling heavy objects, two or more people should operate or use auxiliary
tools such as cranes.
(16) Use suitable load-bearing steel wires and ropes.
(17) When handling high-temperature components, please take corresponding fire
prevention measures.
(18) For the temporary storage of high-temperature components, fire prevention measures
should also be taken.
(19) During the disassembly and inspection process, if it is necessary to heat the heater,
never leave the site.
(20) Use appropriate repair tools.
(21) Regularly calibrate testing instruments, torque wrenches, etc.
(22) After the inspection is completed or during trial operation, the protective cover removed
during the inspection operation should be closed
(23) Tools or parts should be placed on the slide rails of the injection molding machine and
within the range of motion of the machine..
(24) Any resin, hydraulic oil, grease, and water flying off the floor or injection molding
machine should be immediately removed.
(25) If there are any unclear areas during the disassembly process, please contact our
company.

1. Power Supply for Machines and the Installation Requirements for
the Power Supply
Single-phase 3-wire 220V/380VAC 50HZ (Mainland China), 60HZ (Taiwan) (IEE.T/T SYSTEM)
stable AC power supply.
Note: Acceptable voltage range for 220V is 200V~250V.
Acceptable voltage range for 380V is 350V~400V.

2. Electrical Equipment Installation Instructions
(1) The aluminum plate label of the controller must face upwards (with the fan on the top)
to facilitate heat dissipation.
(2) When installing the controller, it should be avoided to be in close proximity to AC
accessories such as circuit breakers, contactors, transformers, etc., to avoid unnecessary
surge interference (in principle, the controller and AC accessories should be placed on
both sides of the bottom plate).
(3) The distance between the controller and the power supply should not be too far, and the
line length should be within 1 meter. The cross-sectional area of 5VDC power line used
for operation should not less than 2.0mm ² to avoid voltage drop affecting the operation.
(4) Each coil of the AC contactor should be connected in parallel with the surge eliminator
provided by our company to eliminate the spark surge caused by frequent operation of
the contactor.
(5) The computer cable (2.5m) connecting the operation panel to the host box cannot be
arbitrarily connected or extended, and should be installed at a location where the
controller can be connected to it.
(6) The electric potential ruler and electric heating wire must be connected with shielded
wires, and the outer network cable must be grounded.
(7) Components such as 220VAC (EMI) power filters must be correctly connected to their
polarity terminals.
(8) All components (including filter and host box) must be grounded to avoid lightning strike
and electrostatic damage.
(9) Input VI, output VO, and other power supply cannot be connected in parallel, including
GI and GO return lines, to avoid component damage caused by eddy currents.
(10) All components that are not within the load specifications of this controller cannot be
85

Operation Manual

installed arbitrarily, including external flow or pressure valves and transformers. (Please
refer to the Appendix List of electrical components)

3. Environmental Conditions for Safe Operation of Electrical
Equipment
The electrical box for installing the controller should have good ventilation, oil and dust
prevention conditions. (Note: If the electric control box is sealed, it is easy to cause the
temperature of the controller to be too high, which will affect normal operation. An exhaust
fan needs to be installed, and the temperature of the electric box should not exceed 65℃.)
The air temperature should be controlled within 5 ℃ - 40 ℃, the relative humidity should
not exceed 50%, and the altitude should be below 1000m.

4. Description of Electrical Safety Protection Device and Interlocking
Device
(1) Safety doors and safety limit switches are designed to ensure the safety of operators and
cannot be modified, moved, disassembled, etc. at will.
(2) If any abnormalities or damaged parts are found in the safety door, please check and
repair them immediately.

5. Safety Operation Instructions for Electrics
(1) When operating the machine, please clearly understand the process before operating.
(2) When hands are wet, do not operate, maintain or repair any electric control device.
(3) After each power on, please wait for 5 seconds to operate the machine. (Including
heating system)
(4) After the power is cut off, please wait for 20 seconds before powering on the machine.

7. Handling, Transportation and Storage of Electrical Equipment
Electrical equipment can be transported and stored within the temperature range of
-40 ℃ to 50 ℃, with a humidity of ≤ 95% (without condensation), and attention should be
paid to rain, dust and mechanical damage. Take measures to prevent moisture, vibration, and
impact to avoid damaging the computer control system of the injection molding machine.

3.2 Mold Base Group
This group is used to set mold plate mold close/open, mold move, functions and other

[Mold Open/Close]

109

 Operation Manual

This page provides mold open/close setting items.
[Description of mold close items]
According to different mold forms, the mold closing process can be fast closing, slow
closing, low pressure, high pressure (mold clamping).
When the mold is closed to the finished product height, it switches to low pressure
protection. When the male and female mold surfaces come into contact with each other,
it switches from low pressure protection to high pressure mold clamping.
The purpose of mold low pressure protection:
1. If there are finished products or foreign objects inside the mold that cannot be
completely detached, failure to detect them during mold closing will result in
damage to the mold.
2. During the mold closing process, if the operator accidentally enters the mold closing
area, the low pressure protection of the mold can prevent the operator from being
injured and reduce the damage or loss.


Mold close pressure: Set the closing pressure for each section, with fast and slow
closing pressures to drive the mold plate movement. The pressure in the
low-pressure mold closing section should be low to achieve mold protection
function. High pressure mold locking is mainly based on the pressure required for
the set locking force. Excessive pressure will cause excessive load on the mechanical
structure and waste of energy.



Mold Close speed: set the mold closing speed of each section.
110

 Operation Manual



Mold close position: set the mold closing position of each section, and set a
reasonable switching position according to the die characteristics



Mold Protect Time: Start timing when entering the low pressure mold closing
process. If exceeded, an alarm will be triggered to avoid damaging the mold.



Mold Clamp time: Start timing when entering the mold clamping process to limit
the mold closing time. If exceeded, an alarm will be triggered.



Mold Close Total time: start timing after entering the mold closing process to limit
the total time of mold closing process, and an alarm will be triggered if the time is
exceeded.

[Description of mold opening items]
Mold opening process can be pressure relief, slow opening,

opening, and

low-speed opening. Firstly, release the high pressure by mode clamping. Then enter the
stages of slow opening, fast opening, and low-speed opening to separate the finished
product from the female mold so as to avoid damaging the finished product. The slow
opening speed should not be too high. After the finished product is separated from the
female mold, the mold can be quickly opened to the distance of the finished product to
the ejector pin, and the mold plate can be stopped after low-speed opening.
When using a robotic arm to pick up finished products, special attention should be paid
to the distance between fast and low-speed opening. If the distance between fast and
slow opening is too small, it may cause the mold plate to stop in an unstable position
due to mechanical inertia, and also cause strong impact on the machine.


Pressure release time: The time for releasing pressure before mold opening. Setting
is too small may cause damage to the finished product during mold opening.



Mold opening pressure: Set the pressure for each section of the mold opening. Slow
opening may require a large amount of pressure to separate the finished product
from the mold, while the pressure for other sections is mainly to drive the mold
plate to move smoothly.



Mold opening speed: Set the mold opening speed for each section. To avoid impact
caused by excessive speed when entering the buffer, the speed of fast mold opening
should not be too high.



Mold opening position: Set the switching position for each section of the mold
opening according to the characteristics of the mold.



Mold open total time: Start timing when entering the mold opening process to limit
the total mold opening time. If exceeded, an alarm will be triggered.



Mold adjustment and mold opening: slow mold opening pressure and speed in the
mold adjustment mode.



Mold adjustment and mold closing: slow mold closing pressure and speed in mold
adjustment mode.

1. Time switching: If the injection time is greater than the set time, the injection

[Setting of blockage position for injection overflow]
During injection, plastic may not be able to enter the mold cavity smoothly due to cold
slag blockage, resulting in injection blockage.
At this point, the endpoint position of the injection will be much farther back than the
positions of other mold shot.
When the mold is not tightly closed, there is no resistance in the mold cavity during
injection, and the injection screw can continue to move forward, squeezing the plastic
into the mold cavity and causing overflow. The injection endpoint position will advance
much further than the positions of other mold shot.
The endpoint position of the injection can be used to monitor blockage or overflow
situations. In the injection holding pressure page, there is a ok product testing group (the
monitoring main switch needs to be turned on before the quality monitoring page, and
various monitoring functions will take effect), which will monitor the injection endpoint
position.

(Number of demands for good products x cycle time) / (number of holes per mold).



PD Remain Time: estimate production remain time from
(Number of demands for good products - number of good products) x (cycle time) /
(number of cavities per mold)



Good Quantity: After executing [start production] and starting the monitoring of
good products, the number of mold shot that meets each good products conditions

x (number of cavities per mold) is the number of qualified products.


NG Quantity: After executing [start production] and starting the monitoring of
defective products, the number of mold shot that fails to meet the conditions of



PD Quantity: After executing [start production], the total number of production
obtained by executing the number of production mold shot x (number of cavities
per mold)



PD Approach QTY.: Yield, (Good Quantity) / (PD Quantity).

[Quality Monitor]

determining good and NG products.
